title: Global Recession Risk Update — Apr 1, 2026
summary: JPMorgan raised its US recession probability to 60% — the highest of any major investment bank — as Liberation Day approached its one-year anniversary with $166 billion in IEEPA tariff revenue under legal threat and the tariff regime widely assessed as having failed its own metrics. China's NBS Manufacturing PMI surged back to expansion at 50.4 in March (first expansion since March 2025), though input prices hit a 4-year high from Iran-conflict commodity costs. President Trump opened his first China visit since 2017 on April 1 in Beijing, with the Trump-Xi summit focusing on agriculture purchases, rare earth concessions, and fentanyl enforcement amid China's record $213.6 billion trade surplus. Markets rallied on Iran ceasefire signals (S&P +0.7%, Brent crude falling to $98.79/barrel, 10Y yield retreating to ~4.30%), while Conference Board Consumer Confidence Expectations remained at 70.9 — the 14th consecutive month below the 80-point recession-warning threshold. Core PCE re-accelerated to 3.06% year-over-year in January 2026 BEA data (released March 13).

title: Global Recession Risk Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Stagflation risk surged in the final week of March as recession probability estimates approached multi-year highs: Moody's Analytics at 48.6%, Goldman Sachs at 30%, J.P. Morgan at 35%, and EY-Parthenon at 40%. The US 10-year Treasury yield reached 4.46% on March 28 — its highest since July 2025 — as bond markets fully priced out 2026 Fed rate cuts and began pricing a 50% probability of a hike by October. University of Michigan consumer sentiment deteriorated to a final March reading of 53.3, with year-ahead inflation expectations surging to 3.8% (the largest monthly jump since April 2025). On March 26, the EU Parliament advanced the 'Turnberry' EU-US trade framework 417-154, while China opened two retaliatory trade investigations into US practices (March 27) in response to the USTR Section 301 probes. The S&P 500 posted its fifth consecutive weekly loss — the longest streak since 2022 — and the US dollar recorded its best month since July 2025 as safe-haven demand surged.

title: Global Recession Risk Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Recession risk escalated sharply in the week of March 19–26, with Wall Street probability estimates reaching 30–49%: Moody's Analytics at 48.6%, EY Parthenon at 40%, J.P. Morgan at 35%, and Goldman Sachs at 30%. The Federal Reserve held rates steady at 3.50–3.75% on March 18, with the dot plot projecting only one cut in 2026 and core PCE revised up to 2.7% year-end, complicating any near-term easing. Consumer sentiment hit its lowest reading of 2026 (University of Michigan 55.5, 2nd percentile historically) as gasoline price shock from the Iran conflict erased earlier gains. On March 11, USTR launched sweeping Section 301 investigations into excess manufacturing capacity across 16 economies — including China, EU, Mexico, and India — targeting tariff imposition by July 2026, while Penn Wharton confirmed the current effective US tariff rate at 10.3%, the highest since 1943. Analysis published March 19 concluded that by the administration's own metrics, the tariff regime has failed to narrow the trade deficit or make foreign producers pay, with JPMorgan estimating pass-through to US consumers accelerating in 2026.
